content.text:
<user_query>
@Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/pages/OnboardingRegistrationCompletePage.tsx:71-73 we arrive at this page after submitting the certification, inquiry, registration, and company information. This should be persisted in local storage as soon as we complete the entire onboarding flow — the payload of the current stored session.

The stored information should include that we finished the onboarding. Each time we try to load the onboarding again, we should be redirected to this onboarding registration complete page. 

 if we want to go through the entire onboarding flow again, we just need to reset the currently stored data. Please add a "Reset" link next to the backlink and make sure the payload can be completely erased.

After erasing, redirect back to the root page. @Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/features/onboarding/AnonymousOnboardingFlow.tsx 
</user_query>

Exploring how onboarding state is stored and where the flow completes so we can persist completion, redirect repeat visits, and add a Reset action.

[REDACTED]

Checking how the certification store uses localStorage and whether other keys need clearing.

[REDACTED]

Implementing: moving completion persistence to localStorage with a full snapshot, redirecting `/welcome/start` when complete, and adding a reset that clears all onboarding-related keys.

[REDACTED]

Fixing the bug in `parsePayload` and locating where to insert the redirect in `AnonymousOnboardingFlow` (after all hooks, before any conditional return).

[REDACTED]

input.description: Run typecheck/lint for onboarding frontend app
TypeScript build to verify no compile errors

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### 1. `onboardingRegistrationCompleteSession.ts`
- **Persistence** moved from `sessionStorage` to **`localStorage`** so completion survives reloads and new tabs.
- Completion payload now includes:
  - **`onboardingCompleted: true`**
  - **`completedAt`** (ISO timestamp)
  - **`flowStateSnapshot`** — full anonymous onboarding state (`flowState`) at submit time
  - **`certificationStoreRaw`** — raw string from `pt1:certification-request-store` at submit time  
- **Legacy**: Old `{ email, org, count }` blobs are still accepted (with snapshots `null`), and one-time **migration** copies legacy **`sessionStorage`** entries into **localStorage**.
- **`clearAnonymousOnboardingStorage()`** clears:
  - `pt1:onboarding-registration-complete`
  - `pt1:onboarding-flow-state`
  - `pt1:certification-request-store`
  - plus the legacy sessionStorage entry  
- Exported **`ONBOARDING_FLOW_STORAGE_KEY`** / **`ONBOARDING_CERTIFICATION_STORE_STORAGE_KEY`** (aligned with `AnonymousOnboardingFlow`).

### 2. `AnonymousOnboardingFlow.tsx`
- On mount, if **`readOnboardingRegistrationCompletePayload()`** returns data, render **`<Navigate to="/registratie-voltooid" replace />`** so `/welcome/start` cannot reopen the wizard after a finished flow.
- On **Versturen**, **`writeOnboardingRegistrationCompletePayload`** is called with **`flowState`** and the current certification store string from **localStorage**.

### 3. `OnboardingRegistrationCompletePage.tsx`
- **Reset** control beside **Terug**: runs **`clearAnonymousOnboardingStorage()`**, then **`navigate("/", { replace: true })`** so you land on **`/`** (anonymous users still get **`RootRedirect`** → **`/welcome`**).

Build: `bun run build` in `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` completes successfully.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Summary of changes:

- Imported **`Button`** from `@procertus-ui/ui`.
- **Terug**: `<Button asChild variant="link" className="text-sm text-muted-foreground"><Link to="/">Terug</Link></Button>` so routing stays on `Link` with Radix `Slot` semantics.
- **Reset**: `<Button type="button" variant="link" className="text-sm text-muted-foreground" onClick={…}>Reset</Button>`.

`variant="link"` applies the design-system link styling (underline on hover, accent typography); `text-sm text-muted-foreground` keeps the softer secondary look you had before. Build completes successfully.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
And now use both buttons in a button group.
</user_query>

              type="button"
              variant="link"
              className="text-sm text-muted-foreground"
              onClick={() => {
                clearAnonymousOnboardingStorage();
                navigate("/", { replace: true });
              }}
            >
              Reset
            </Button>
          </ButtonGroup>

@Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/user-authentication/LoginForm.stories.tsx @Procertus-ui-experiment/packages/ui-lib/src/user-authentication/LoginForm.tsx  make a new variation of the login form to use passwordless login, providing only an email address.

Adding a `variant` prop (`password` | `passwordless`) and updating the form and stories.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what was added.

### `LoginForm.tsx`
- **`variant`**: `"password"` (default) or **`"passwordless"`**.
- **Passwordless UI**: only the email field; no password field, no “Forgot your password?”.
- **`onSubmit`**: `(data: { email: string; password?: string }) => void` — in passwordless mode it is called with **`{ email }` only**; with password, **`{ email, password }`** as before.
- **`submitLabel`**: optional override. Default is **`"Sign in"`** for password and **`"Send sign-in link"`** for passwordless (magic-link style).

Exported **`LoginFormVariant`** alongside **`LoginFormProps`**.

### `LoginForm.stories.tsx`
- **`Passwordless`** — email filled.
- **`PasswordlessWithError`** — general error + email field error.
- **`PasswordlessSubmitting`** — loading state.

Existing stories stay **`variant: "password"`** by default, so **`SignupPage`** and **`AuthLayout`** stories are unchanged.

Here’s what changed:

### `SignupPage.tsx`
- **Layout** matches the Storybook `AuthLayout` pattern: default **`card` shell** (no `card={false}`), with **`title`** and **`description`** on the left.
- **Panel** uses the same art setup as the story: `panel={{ image: "/auth-carousel-1.png", title, subtitle }}` (hero image + right-hand copy). The file is already at `apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/public/auth-carousel-1.png`, so the URL is `/auth-carousel-1.png` like in Storybook.
- **Children**: **`LoginForm`** with **`variant="passwordless"`**, Dutch **`submitLabel="Inloggen"`**, and controlled **`email`**. Submit looks up a **mock user by e‑mail** and calls **`login(user.id)`**, then **`navigate("/requests")`** (replacing the old `/app` hop). Unknown e‑mails get a clear Dutch error.
- The **“Nieuw bij PROCERTUS?”** card with **Start certificatieaanvraag** is still there under the form, with `mt-section` so it sits below the form in the card content.

The centered **H1 / H3 / lead** block was removed because **AuthLayout** now provides the main **title** and **description** in the card header, in line with the `AuthLayout` stories.

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented:

### `AuthLayout.tsx`
- New optional prop **`belowCard?: React.ReactNode`** — rendered **under** the main card in the left column.
- With **`card={true}`** (default): order is **Card → `belowCard`**, wrapped in a fragment, with **`gap-section`** on the parent stack (same spacing as before between login card and secondary panel).
- With **`card={false}`**: **`children`** then **`belowCard`**, so the slot still works for non-card layouts.

### `SignupPage.tsx`
- **`LoginForm`** is the only **`children`** (inside the primary card body).
- The **“Nieuw bij PROCERTUS?”** block is passed as **`belowCard={...}`** (removed `mt-section` on that card; spacing comes from **`gap-section`** on the layout).

`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` **`bun run build`** completes successfully.

input.description: Lint new prototype login form files

content.text:
Here’s what was added and wired up.

### `MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm` (`packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/mock-prototype-auth/MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm.tsx`)
- Renders **`MockPrototypeUserSelect`** in a labeled field (with Dutch **`FieldDescription`**).
- Renders **`LoginForm`** from **`@procertus-ui/ui-lib`** with **`variant="passwordless"`**.
- Keeps **`email`** in sync with the selected prototype user (`useEffect` on `selectedUserId` / `users`).
- On submit: if nothing is selected, shows **`pickerRequiredMessage`** (default: *"Kies een prototypegebruiker om verder te gaan."*); otherwise **`login(selectedUserId)`** and **`onLoggedIn?.()`**.
- **`submitLabel`**, **`isSubmitting`**, and **`className`** are supported.

### Exports (`packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src/index.ts`)
- **`MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm`** and **`MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginFormProps`**.

### Storybook (`mock-prototype-passwordless-login-form.stories.tsx`)
- Same **`MockPrototypeAuthProvider` + sample users** pattern as **`mock-prototype-user-select.stories.tsx`**, with a small session readout after “sign in”.

### `SignupPage.tsx`
- Uses **`MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm`** with **`submitLabel="Inloggen"`** and **`onLoggedIn={() => navigate("/requests", { replace: true })}`**.
- Drops local email/error/login wiring; copy updated to mention choosing a prototype user.

`apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding` **`bun run build`** completes successfully.

content.text:
Here’s what was added to **`AuthLayout`**:

### `notice?: React.ReactNode`
- Optional copy shown **under** `description` in the card header.
- Rendered in a **`div`** with **`mt-2 text-xs leading-relaxed text-muted-foreground`** so it reads as a short, muted hint under the main description.
- Like **`description`**, it’s only used when **`card`** is true (default).

### Storybook
- **`LoginWithNotice`** demonstrates **`notice`** alongside the existing **`Login`** story.

You can pass strings or richer **`ReactNode`** content via **`notice`** (e.g. set **`notice`** on **`SignupPage`**’s **`AuthLayout`** for prototype hints).

input.new_string:
<MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm
        submitLabel="Inloggen"
        onLoggedIn={() => navigate("/requests", { replace: true })}
      />
input.old_string:
<LoginForm
        variant="passwordless"
        submitLabel="Inloggen"
        email={email}
        onEmailChange={(value) => {
          setEmail(value);
          setError(undefined);
        }}
        error={error}
        onSubmit={({ email: submitted }) => {
          const normalized = submitted.trim().toLowerCase();
          const user = users.find((u) => u.email.toLowerCase() === normalized);
          if (!user) {
            setError(
              "Geen prototype-gebruiker voor dit e-mailadres. Gebruik het adres van een beschikbare demo-gebruiker.",
            );
            return;
          }
          setError(undefined);
          login(user.id);
          navigate("/requests", { replace: true });
        }}
      />

input.description: Verify build after prototype color tokens

content.text:
Here’s what was added.

### `packages/ui/src/styles/themes/default.css`
Semantic tokens next to **`muted`** / **`muted-foreground`**:

**`:root` (light)**  
- `--prototype: var(--brand-accent-100)` — soft teal surface (same idea as muted using a neutral surface)  
- `--prototype-foreground: var(--brand-accent-900)` — text on that surface  

**`.dark`**  
- `--prototype: var(--brand-accent-950)`  
- `--prototype-foreground: var(--brand-accent-200)`  

### `packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css` (`@theme inline`)
Right after the muted entries:

```css
--color-prototype: var(--prototype);
--color-prototype-foreground: var(--prototype-foreground);
```

Tailwind can use **`bg-prototype`**, **`text-prototype-foreground`**, **`border-prototype`**, etc.

### `MockPrototypePasswordlessLoginForm.tsx`
The Demo badge now uses **`bg-prototype text-prototype-foreground`** so it follows these tokens.

If you want the badge to stay **solid accent-700 + white**, change only the `:root` pair to e.g. `--prototype: var(--brand-accent-700)` and `--prototype-foreground: var(--neutral-white)` (and tune dark mode similarly).

content.text:
Here’s what was implemented.

### `vatPrototypePresets.ts`

- **`CompanyFormFieldKey`** – the six company fields that can be prefilled.
- **`companyFormFieldsPrefilledByMockLookup`** – uses **`enrichRegistrationContext`** and returns which of those keys end up non-empty for the chosen preset + e-mail (same rules as the real load).
- **`companyFormFieldsResolvedThroughLookupStep`** – maps **completed** simulation steps (`0`…`4`, aligned with `vatLookupSimulationStepsForPreset`) to fields the copy treats as “done” in the story:
  - **prefix** → `country` when the VAT prefix (or scenario fallback) yields a country  
  - **registry** → `organizationName` when the mock register has a name  
  - **entity** → address parts that exist on the mock  
  - **email** → full set from **`companyFormFieldsPrefilledByMockLookup`** (register + domain, as in the real enrich)

### `AnonymousOnboardingFlow.tsx`

- **`CompanyPrefillFormSkeleton`** – same grid as the real form (2 columns + 2×2 address block):
  - **Prefilled** fields: `Skeleton` bars; still “loading” = `animate-pulse`, **resolved in the current simulation** = calmer `bg-muted/70` (no pulse).
  - **Not** in the prefilled set: dashed **“Handmatig invullen”** placeholder (no skeleton).
- While **`companyLookupPhase === "loading"`**, the block is shown under the progress list.  
- **`completedCompanySimulationStepIndex`** is derived from **`lookupStepIndex`**, **`lookupProgress`**, and **`companyLookupPhase`** (when progress is 100 or phase is `ready`, it’s treated as step `4` so all expected fields look resolved just before the real form appears).

Build and oxlint both pass.

Tracing how prototype color tokens are defined and whether the onboarding app loads them (Tailwind content paths and CSS imports).

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: Tailwind v4 only generates utilities for classes found in `@source` globs. `globals.css` scans `packages/ui/src` and `packages/ui-lib/src`, but not `packages/ui-pt1-prototype/src`. `PrototypeCard` lives in `ui-pt1-prototype`, so `border-prototype`, `bg-prototype`, and `text-prototype-foreground` aren't emitted in the app bundle—Storybook still matches because its Vite root/story paths differ or stories pull in classes differently. Adding `@source` for `ui-pt1-prototype` in shared `globals.css` so all apps include those utilities.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Verify onboarding app build after Tailwind @source fix
Confirm prototype utility classes in built CSS

content.text:
## Cause

`PrototypeCard` uses Tailwind utilities such as `border-prototype`, `bg-prototype`, and `text-prototype-foreground`. Those map to `--color-prototype` / `--prototype` in `@procertus-ui/ui/globals.css`, which is fine.

With **Tailwind v4**, classes are only generated for sources listed in **`@source`**. In `packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css` you already had:

- `@source ".."` → `packages/ui/src`
- `@source "../../../ui-lib/src"` → `packages/ui-lib`

There was **no** scan of `packages/ui-pt1-prototype`, where `PrototypeCard.tsx` lives. The onboarding app only had `@source "./**/*.{ts,tsx}"` in its own `index.css`, so it never saw those class strings. Result: utilities like `.border-prototype` were **missing** from the built CSS. You still saw a dashed border because `border-2 border-dashed` applied, but the **color** utility was absent so the border fell back to the default (gray). The Demo badge and title also lost their prototype colors for the same reason.

Storybook for `ui-pt1-prototype` still looked correct because its build context / scanned files differed, so those utilities were often present there.

## Fix

Add the prototype package to the shared Tailwind scan list in `globals.css` (same pattern as `ui-lib`):

```12:14:packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css
@source "../../../ui-lib/src";
/* PT1 prototype package (PrototypeCard, mock auth): prototype / prototype-foreground utilities */
@source "../../../ui-pt1-prototype/src";
```

After this change, the onboarding production CSS includes `border-prototype`, `bg-prototype`, and `text-prototype-foreground` (verified in `dist/assets/*.css`).

**Note:** `PrototypeCard` does not set a tinted **card** background—only the border, badge, and title use prototype colors. Any pale wash in Storybook is mostly from the default `Card` surface / canvas; the important fix is that prototype **semantic** colors now apply in the app. If you want an explicit wash on the card body, we can add something like `bg-prototype/5` on the `Card` in a follow-up.

[REDACTED]
